mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator simplifies the management of Argo CD instances on Kubernetes by automating the deployment and lifecycle management of Argo CD applications. It is particularly useful for teams adopting GitOps practices, enabling them to manage their Kubernetes resources declaratively. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Helm chart repository for Argo CD.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Argo CD Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace argocd-operator

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.17.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Argo CD Operator.
> Installs the Argo CD Operator in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to manage the scope of the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> Creates the OperatorGroup in the argocd-operator namespace.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription for the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to manage the lifecycle of the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> Creates the Subscription for the Argo CD Operator.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator

```

> Lists the pods in the argocd-operator namespace to verify the installation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if any Argo CD instances are managed by this operator.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator

kubectl delete namespace argocd-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Argo CD Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade the operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n argocd-operator -o yaml > argocd-operator-backup.yaml

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd-operator --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrades the Argo CD Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n argocd-operator -o yaml > argocd-operator-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Subscription" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that OLM is installed and running in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install the operator: unable to retrieve the latest version'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be reachable or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository cache.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may be misconfigured or missing required permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the operator logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd-operator' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found in argocd-operator namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may not have been installed correctly or the namespace may not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify the installation steps and ensure the namespace exists using 'kubectl get namespaces'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved RBAC configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of 'spec.sourceNamespace' in Subscription.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.16.0, remove 'spec.sourceNamespace' from your Subscription manifest.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n argocd-operator'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd-operator
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:

  namespace: argocd-operator
spec:

  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
